clean_body: It was worse than we thought...| | | In Case You Missed It: Attorney General Jason Miyares Releases Report on Virginia Parole Board:‘Worse than we thought’ VIRGINIA MERCURY: At a news conference in Richmond Wednesday announcing the release of an investigative report, Miyares said he and his team had found numerous instances of former Parole Board Chair Adrianne Bennett, who is now serving as a judge in Virginia Beach, falsifying official records. But because state law treats that as a misdemeanor offense, Miyares said, the one-year statute of limitations had already expired by the time his office’s investigation began. RICHMOND TIMES-DISPATCH: Attorney General Jason Miyares said Wednesday that former Virginia Parole Board Chair Adrianne Bennett could have been charged with falsifying parole discharge records, but the alleged violations cannot be prosecuted because the statute of limitations has passed. He also asserted that Bennett “unlawfully suspended” Virginia’s “three strikes” parole ineligibility statute, but Brian Moran, then Virginia’s secretary of public safety, rejected her bid for “a new, lenient interpretation” of the statute. WSET: In 2020, the Virginia Parole Board’s actions came under elevated public scrutiny after it released 95 offenders in March 2020 – the highest number of releases ever granted in a single month. According to the report, the 95 offenders released in March 2020 included: 4 capital murderers, 31 first-degree murderers, 11 rapists, and 33 offenders convicted of robbery. Of the 134 offenders released between March 2020 and April 2020, Miyares said 130 of them were convicted of violent crimes. Only four were non-violent. THE ASSOCIATED PRESS: Republican Attorney General Jason Miyares laid out his allegations against Adrianne Bennett as he outlined the findings of a yearlong investigation by his office into the practices of the board. The probe focused especially on the board’s activities in March and April of 2020, when it granted release to a higher-than-normal number of inmates in what Miyares called a “parole-granting frenzy” carried out with “disregard” for victims. THE PROGRESS-INDEX: The attorney general claimed Bennett ignored the board's "three strikes" parole-ineligibility statute and approved the releases despite being told not to by then-Gov. Ralph Northam's administration and the General Assembly's rejection of her request to repeal the statute. He said she also failed on numerous occasions to notify victims and their families of the inmates' releases. THE WASHINGTON POST: The latest report from Miyares offers more details, charging that 130 of the released inmates had been convicted of violent crimes. He said the parole board failed to properly notify victims or their families 83 times in March and April of that year. The report says the board failed to properly notify local commonwealth’s attorneys 66 times during that period. | Worse than we thought:’ AG Miyares on the full Virginia Parole Board report | | AG Jason Miyares releases Virginia Parole Board report alleging repeated violations by former-chair Virginia state leaders respond to Virginia Parole Board investigation report Virginia AG Jason Miyares releases long-awaited report on Virginia Parole Board Thank you, Team Miyares | | | | A Personal Note from Jason: 50 years ago, my mother escaped Cuba and Fidel Castro’s socialist regime with just the clothes on her back. By the grace of God, she overcame unimaginable odds to become an American. Because of her courage, I went to school, became a prosecutor, served in the Virginia House of Delegates, and made history by becoming Virginia’s first Latino-American Attorney General. Now, the radical socialist ideas my mother escaped in Cuba are uprooting our laws and freedoms, but I will never stop fighting to protect the American Dream. name: Karen Greenhalgh
subject: UPDATED Week 3: Making Virginia a Safer Place for Families
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Karen Greenhalgh for Delegate
body:
| | |
---
| | **Week 3: Making Virginia a Safer Place for Families**
---
| | Despite the lightning-fast pace of the General Assembly session, House Republicans are working diligently to pass legislation that lowers costs, improves our schools, and makes our communities safer. This week, we have already advanced several key pieces of our public safety agenda that will reduce crime and protect Virginians. The legislation includes effective measures to prevent crimes before they happen, provide the necessary tools to law enforcement so they can do their jobs, and keep dangerous criminal and drug activity off our streets. _Stopping Crimes Before They Happen_ _ _ A critical piece of our public safety agenda includes undoing failed policies that passed the Democrat-controlled General Assembly three years ago. For instance, Democrats passed a law in 2020 that took away law enforcement’s ability to conduct various traffic stops that could subsequently reveal more serious offenses – such as possession of illegal firearms or deadly drugs – and allow law enforcement to take action to prevent more heinous crimes from happening. With an increase in traffic-related deaths and fatal drug overdoses over the last two years, local leaders across**** the 85th district and around Virginia have asked for legislation to reverse this policy. Fortunately, House Republicans are advancing legislation ([HB 1380](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/7f7636126a3844c9/06c007b767ce09fcc3d6b838967d376c/37923)) sponsored by our late friend Delegate Ronnie Campbell that restores law enforcement’s ability to do its job and provides the necessary tools to prevent serious crimes from occurring. _Providing Tax Relief_** _ _** Meanwhile, we are delivering on our goal to bring tax relief to Virginians. Earlier this week, we passed a tax relief package ([HB 2319](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/7f7636126a3844c9/925d665261b30b3bc3d6b838967d376c/37923) and [HB 2138](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/7f7636126a3844c9/5902bf72bb04c76cc3d6b838967d376c/37923), McNamara) that will keep more of your hard-earned money in your pockets and better position Virginia to compete for jobs and spur economic growth. These changes will generate economic development, bring in more talent, spur innovation, and encourage more people to establish their roots in Virginia for years to come.
---
| | **My Session Updates**
---
| |
---
| | My first bill presentation for 2023, Election Accuracy HB 1377, passed subcommittee 19-2!
---
| |
---
| | I had the pleasure of meeting with Teresa Pregnall and her team from Concerned Women for America of Virginia.
---
| |
---
| | I spoke to a group from 4H along with Sheriff Stolle and Senator DeSteph. I am so excited about all this next generation of young people are doing!
---
| |
---
| | As a member of the Filipino-American Community Action Group (Fil-Am CAG) in Virginia Beach, I am honored to introduce HB2440. This bill creates the Asian American and Pacific Islander Heritage Commission. The commission will work with the Board of Education to create education standards regarding Asian or Asian American and Pacific Islander contributions, culture, history, heritage, and language.
---
| |
---
| | I had the pleasure of meeting with a number of individuals from Regent University and commending the incredible institution. Thank you for coming out Chris Lambert, Lou Isakoff, Joseph Umidi, Steve Bruce, Bill Hathaway, Martha Smith, and Claire Foster!!
---
| | As the legislative process moves quickly in Richmond, you can easily track the status of my [legislation here](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/7f7636126a3844c9/548ae764c1bc496ac3d6b838967d376c/37923). If you are interested in following any other legislation in the General Assembly, visit [www.lis.virginia.gov](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/7f7636126a3844c9/c679276ec8d5274dc3d6b838967d376c/37923). If I can be of assistance to you or you would like to share your position on any of my legislation, please do not hesitate to reach out to me at delkgreenhalgh@house.virginia.gov or (804) 698-1085. You can also follow me on Twitter @KGreenhalgh85 or like my [Facebook page](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/7f7636126a3844c9/8cd24ae0e76dca0ec3d6b838967d376c/37923) to stay up to date on what I’m working on in Richmond. Have a great weekend,
---
| |
---
| Your Delegate, Karen **Delegate Karen S. Greenhalgh** **House of Delegates** 1-804-698-1085 [karengreenhalgh.com](http://sendpatriot.campstrategic.com/go/1/23a9f17af7861f4f16f79d307c796b93/37923/7f7636126a3844c9/c56ff67f00fc67e1c3d6b838967d376c/37923 "http://firepit.campstrategic.com/go/1/9206fe5164b4fa85041ad8cde91a9694/37923/c1e1fdff1fcd89fe/178744a2ec7ca92cc3d6b838967d376c/37923")
---

name: =?utf-8?Q?Andrew=20Roth?=
subject: Last Day of the Week!
body:
| | | |
---
| | | **Friends,**

You’ve endured a lot of emails from us this week, but I have just one short update!

**TOP STORY**

Imitation is the sincerest form of flatter, right? Well, don’t look now but the liberal wing of the Republican Party wants to copy our success by creating “Main Street” state caucuses. From a Pluribus News [article](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=2de0870ad2&e=64ecec6d03):

_[Republican Main Street Partnership President Sarah] Chamberlain said state lawmakers who have reached out to the group want to establish similar partnerships within their legislatures or work closely together. She sees the effort as a way to groom candidates for federal office as Main Street Republicans seek other offices or retire._

She downplayed the risk of any tension with the U.S. Freedom Caucus and said that most members of the two groups work together and agree more than they disagree. But she stressed that the Main Street Republicans outnumber the U.S. Freedom Caucus Republicans.

She said their goal is to elect more Main Street Republicans in Congress to reduce gridlock.

“It’d be nice to have more people who want to get to ‘yes’ elected,” Chamberlain said. “So that’s what we’re trying to do in identifying the candidates.”

Don’t be fooled, folks. The Republican Main Street Partnership is a liberal-front group. They receive money from labor unions and Michael Bloomberg. And their members in Congress used to include Adam Kinzinger and Liz Cheney.

The best way we can fight off the liberal wing of the GOP is to grow the Network. That means we need generous support from YOU. Please donate now to help fund our effort. Better yet, please sign up for a recurring donation so we have a reliable monthly cash flow to finance our operations. Don’t let the Liz Cheney Republicans beat us!

---
| [CLICK HERE TO DONATE](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=2cf6c1c72c&e=64ecec6d03 "CLICK HERE TO DONATE")
---
|
**Keep fighting,**

**Andy Roth**
_President
State Freedom Caucus Network_

P.S. Your help is critical in building out a network of patriots and freedom fighters throughout our nation’s state capitols and in your home state. Please consider a [contribution](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=9057befef7&e=64ecec6d03) and join us as we work to advance the cause of liberty.
---
| [ ](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=abb51b2e8a&e=64ecec6d03)
---
| | |
---
| _Copyright © 2023 State Freedom Caucus Network, All rights reserved._
You are receiving this email because you opted in via our website.

**Our mailing address is:**
State Freedom Caucus Network300 Independence Ave SEWashington, DC 20003-1021
[Add us to your address book](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/vcard?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=7256df9da1)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/profile?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=7256df9da1&e=64ecec6d03&c=39fb27378e) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://statefreedomcaucus.us20.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=a363d70ccaaebc9e5732e6783&id=7256df9da1&e=64ecec6d03&c=39fb27378e).

name: Cheri Beasley
subject: Join me in supporting the Council for a Livable World
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Cheri Beasley for North Carolina
body:
#
Friends -
The Council for a Livable World endorsed my campaign in this election cycle. Their endorsement was important to me, and I want to take this opportunity to tell you about their mission and current work.
The Council’s chief goal is to reduce and eventually eliminate the threat posed by nuclear weapons. In pursuit of that goal, the Council helps elect candidates who support their mission and then supports those members once in Congress. This includes pushing for diplomacy-first foreign policy, nuclear arms control, and common-sense defense spending in order to create a more livable world.
They are circulating a petition to President Biden calling for continued negotiations for arms control agreements in the future. The war in Ukraine has once again made the threat caused by nuclear weapons all too clear. In this context, continuing negotiations with other nuclear-armed states is essential even when it seems difficult.
[**Would you add your name to the petition to show your support for continued efforts to increase arms control agreements and prevent a new nuclear arms race?**](https://act.cheribeasley.com/go/5945?t=1&akid=2282%2E357359%2EWmfWSS)
The Council raised almost $1 million for 38 endorsed candidates this cycle. They will support newly elected candidates in the 118th Congress to advance these policy goals.
Organizations that combine strategic election interventions with a thoughtful approach and important insight are key to making an impact in the Washington policy conversation.
[**In addition to the petition, please consider joining their supporter list or making a financial contribution to their important work.**](https://act.cheribeasley.com/go/5946?t=2&akid=2282%2E357359%2EWmfWSS)
Sincerely,
Cheri Beasley

Paid For By Cheri Beasley for North Carolina
Cheri Beasley for North Carolina
PO Box 28778
Raleigh, NC 27611
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you want to receive fewer emails, you may [submit this form to get only our most important updates](https://act.cheribeasley.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=2282%2E357359%2EWmfWSS). Emails like this are a critical tool for our campaign's fundraising and organizing efforts, but if you need to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.cheribeasley.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=2282%2E357359%2EWmfWSS). If you would like to support Cheri's campaign for the U.S. Senate in North Carolina, please consider [making a donation](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beasley-em-2021?refcode=em-footer&t=5&refcode2=2282_357359_WmfWSS&akid=2282%2E357359%2EWmfWSS) today to help us make history.

name: Adam Schiff
subject: Right Matters. Truth Matters. Decency Matters. Democracy Matters.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/62?t=1001&akid=1712%2E5257348%2EjNmA6N ]Adam Schiff for Senate

Derek,

For more than a decade, Donald Trump has poisoned our body politic with
relentless lies, fabrications, and false smears. Trust me, I experienced
all of them.

From his xenophobic and false claims about President Obama’s birth country
to the Big Lie about the 2020 election, Trump has made lying in Republican
politics the new normal. In a party dominated by his MAGA cult of
personality, it is the only way to advance.

And that is one of the reasons why I am running for the United States
Senate representing California.

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1312?c0=jan2023_abs_em_launch._tD9LH&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_LAU_RES_AC_20230127_AM2_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1004&akid=1712%2E5257348%2EjNmA6N ]If you’re with me on this journey to represent California in the
Senate, please pitch in $10 to become a Founding Donor of Schiff 2024
today.

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1312?c0=jan2023_abs_em_launch._tD9LH&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_LAU_RES_AC_20230127_AM2_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1005&akid=1712%2E5257348%2EjNmA6N ]DONATE $10

Because right matters. Because truth matters. Because decency matters.
Because democracy matters.

Nowhere are these values under greater assault than in a United States
Senate paralyzed by an archaic, unconstitutional filibuster rule that
Mitch McConnell and the GOP use to stifle Democratic legislation.

Where House-passed legislation on voting rights, campaign finance reform,
and an assault weapons ban have withered on the vine.

And where Republicans have blocked our efforts to create an economy that
works for everyone, where the rich pay their fair share, and everyone has
a shot to succeed.

Yes, we need to bring serious change to the Senate. And I intend to be a
fierce advocate not just for California, but for the nation — standing up
against the MAGA movement. Just like I’ve done for years.

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1312?c0=jan2023_abs_em_launch._tD9LH&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_LAU_RES_AC_20230127_AM2_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1006&akid=1712%2E5257348%2EjNmA6N ]So I'm asking you, Derek, to pitch in and become a Founding
Donor of Schiff 2024 as I run for Senate – for California and the nation.

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1072?c0=jan2023_abs_em_launch.yaxvcj&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_LAU_RES_AC_20230127_AM2_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1007&akid=1712%2E5257348%2EjNmA6N ]EXPRESS DONATE $3

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1015?c0=jan2023_abs_em_launch.U6B5wJ&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_LAU_RES_AC_20230127_AM2_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1008&akid=1712%2E5257348%2EjNmA6N ]EXPRESS DONATE $10

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1016?c0=jan2023_abs_em_launch.aZ7DO3&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_LAU_RES_AC_20230127_AM2_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1009&akid=1712%2E5257348%2EjNmA6N ]EXPRESS DONATE $25

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1017?c0=jan2023_abs_em_launch.sfJsy4&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_LAU_RES_AC_20230127_AM2_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1010&akid=1712%2E5257348%2EjNmA6N ]EXPRESS DONATE $50

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1018?c0=jan2023_abs_em_launch.0yUkhh&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_LAU_RES_AC_20230127_AM2_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1011&akid=1712%2E5257348%2EjNmA6N ]EXPRESS DONATE $100

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=jan2023_abs_em_launch.3OtEFQ&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_LAU_RES_AC_20230127_AM2_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1012&akid=1712%2E5257348%2EjNmA6N ]ANOTHER AMOUNT

It’s absolutely critical that we get off to a fast start in these first 24
hours, so thanks for whatever you’re able to spare. I couldn’t do this
without you.

— Adam

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1312?c0=jan2023_abs_em_launch._tD9LH&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_LAU_RES_AC_20230127_AM2_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1013&akid=1712%2E5257348%2EjNmA6N ]Adam Schiff for Senate Banner

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1631?c0=jan2023_abs_em_launch.m1gE9u&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_LAU_RES_AC_20230127_AM2_V1_S1_VSP&email=&t=1014&akid=1712%2E5257348%2EjNmA6N ]DONATE MONTHLY

150 E. Olive Ave., Suite 208 Burbank CA, 91502

name: local signatures needed [Left Action]
subject: Protect our seniors: Say no to cuts to Social Security and Medicare
body:
| | | | |
---
| | | [Tell Republicans: Hands off our Social Security and Medicare! >>](https://default.salsalabs.org/Te5c44d03-7922-4b5b-a445-baa903905f49/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T5c895055-27c9-4743-8696-bddf03cccecb/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | Republicans have been trying for years to strip seniors of the benefits they’ve worked hard to pay into their entire lives. And now that the GOP has control of the House, Social Security and Medicare are on the chopping block. [House Republicans are planning on making cuts to these essential benefit programs as part of their fight over the debt ceiling.](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tcad6c867-88dd-475b-b572-38f77ed47c1d/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) They’re putting our seniors at risk because Republicans don’t want to pay their own bills [(](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb574d3dd-9186-4f5a-b1a8-059a6e2f0f3d/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)[25% of America’s national debt was racked up under Trump](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0647b47a-f682-4f19-b2bf-0b47b01c3d8b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)[)!](https://default.salsalabs.org/T22849b9f-2305-4c97-800b-1c4a46bc559c/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) We can’t let that happen! President Biden has promised to use his veto power to stop Republicans if they so much as try to cut Social Security and Medicare. But we still need to keep up the pressure, and send a message loud and clear to Republicans: [Hands off our Social Security and Medicare!! Add your name now >>](https://default.salsalabs.org/T9c123d25-9b93-4c3c-815b-27671fba0c31/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

name: Senator Amanda Chase
subject: Week 3: What Happened this Week in the General Assembly?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase
body:
From the Desk of Senator Chase
---
| | | | | | [ ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001O_3xN4dqBUhZuAeTJmlhpeRKnEFt51eylFBmgLz9tfxz2wmMLS1LtKT0d7SLKsrpNYfENNLnCiKDmyDb7h-lSq5tJMdLxLFC3ZH1icnHZrn4bgxQb-wqlnqFbzXyhmLUfQXJzqiPz3onTJmPA85VsTHx60WhVUG7wfhMZI_i02o=&c=Go79gCVtAt9ULHm7nIFkC4to6kXP2kB9LUs6Y97Ce7ZBtS6lPDLA0w==&ch=8dQwsMuU7Buw7806-FRsgF-I1yxU9RVnMo-n-rkJ-w68w7TleYxe6Q==)
---
| CUT TO THE CHASE WITH SENATOR CHASE Weekly Session Newsletter Week 3 of 7
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| |
---
| WHAT HAPPENED THIS WEEK?
---
| WEEK 3: GENERAL ASSEMBLY UPDATE
---
| |
---
| Senator Amanda Chase
---
Senator Chase has represented Chesterfield, Colonial Heights and Amelia Counties in the Senate since first elected in 2015, almost 8 years.
She has been rated as the most conservative Senator in Virginia by the American Conservative Union and has a lifetime score of 100% with the Family Foundation, VCDL and A with the NRA.
She is also a champion in the General Assembly for pushing back against vaccine and mask mandates, calling for a full investigation into the 2020 Presidential Election, parental rights in education, medical freedom and help for our veterans.
---
| 2023 SESSION WEEK 3 OVERVIEW
Dear Friends, After 2 years of outrunning Covid, it finally caught up with me. Sunday night I tested positive for Covid, but thanks to Dr. Sheila Furey I was able to immediately take Ivermectin. By Thursday I was fully back and on the Senate floor voting against bad gun bills.
Thanks to quick acting Ivermectin, I only missed 3 days of session. I was able to keep up by watching online as my team held down the office in my absence. One of my bills that died in Senate Health and Education was a bill that would allow all Virginians to have access to Ivermectin by allowing doctors to be doctors without fear of retribution or losing their license. We need to give Virginians more medical freedom, not less.
In this week's newsletter, I provide an overview of the third week of the 2023 General Assembly Session and give you the inside scoop on what really happened this week. Look for a weekly update each Saturday morning.
In each weekly newsletter, we'll discuss the most notable General Assembly news of the week. I'll give you an update on bills I've sponsored along with how they fared our Democrat controlled Senate, which is now 22 to 18, and highlight other bills that might be of interest to you.
I also want to teach you how to participate in the process. You'll learn how to identify where to find the bills that are before the General Assembly and learn how to track them. You'll learn how to comment or speak on bills so that you can engage in the legislative process.
With only 46 days to review roughly 2,000 bills, legislation moves very quickly through the process and can be difficult to track and follow even for the most experienced legislative watchdog.
I will also let you know about upcoming events, with exciting news about my re-election campaign, which is already well under way this year. Find out how you can help my campaign while I'm busy doing the work of the people. We have plenty of opportunities to volunteer.
---
| GOP EVENTS
---
| CHESTERFIELD GOP BREAKFAST
---
| |
---
|
---

Saturday morning, Mike & I attended the Chesterfield GOP or CCRC Monthly Breakfast at the Wood and Iron Game Day Restaurant and Bar in Chesterfield County with Team Chase.

Great to see Congressman Rob Wittman, Republican nominee Leon Benjamin and so many Republicans coming together to sign each others petitions to get our names on the ballot.

If you live in Congressional District 4, be sure to get out and vote in the Special Election on February 21 at your regular polling location.

It was a packed house with over 23 candidates present and who will be on the ballot this June 20 and November 7.

Better Business Bureau President and CEO Barry Moore was the Special Guest. We learned the importance of not getting scammed by con artists.

##### Incredible turnout at the Chesterfield GOP Business meeting Monday night. Thanks to all who came to join and effect change. We need to grow the GOP so we can have more boots on the ground come Election Day and ensure Chesterfield stays red.

Thanks to my surrogate speaker Jody who spoke on my behalf Monday night.
A huge thanks to everyone who signed my petition Monday night to get my name on the ballot. We need a minimum of 250 valid signatures to get my name on the ballot.

SB1017 would have given us permanent daylight savings time in Virginia. I would definitely support this bill sponsored by Senator Richard Stuart.

The bill failed to pass the Senate on an 18 to 21 vote.

SJ255 enshrines abortion in our state constitution and is a terrible idea. The bill is copatroned by Senators McClellan and Boysko.
This resolution came before the Senate Privileges & Elections subcommittee today and will come up in the Full Senate Privileges and Elections next Tuesday 15 minutes after session in the Capital in Senate Room 3.
Abortion is not a constitutional right. Passes 3 to 1 to the full Senate Privileges & Elections Committee

Senator Ebbin's SJ242 would enshrine Same Sex Marriage in the Constitution through a Constitutional Amendment.
This bill also passed out of today's Senate Privilege and Elections Subcommittee and will also come before the full Senate Privileges and Elections Committee next Tuesday 15 minutes after session in the Capitol in Senate Room 3.
I will be a NO vote on this Amendment.
Thankfully SJ235 failed to pass out of the Senate Privileges and Elections Subcommittee.
Many good Republicans have been deceived into believing Convention of States is a good way to rein in our federal government. Unfortunately, in reality it is a Trojan Horse.
While I support term limits and balance budget amendments, calling a Convention of States would be the end of our Constitution.
Do you really think only conservatives would be chosen as commissioners?
It's naive to believe that only conservatives would be able to amend our U.S. Constitution. Convention of States creates appointed, not elected representation, basically creating an unelected shadow government of unelected representatives that are not accountable to the people.
I know how Republican Conventions work. They don't work.
They are controlled by political consultants who manipulate and control the process.
Convention of States, otherwise called an Article V Convention won't work either. Many conservatives think this is a good idea but they can only speak in theory because they themselves have not been elected and don't understand that politics doesn't always work out as we learned in theory in government class.
I am one of 4 Virginian Representatives to serve as a commissioner to the first ever simulated Convention of States in Jamestown several years ago. I can speak from experience as few legislators and so called experts cannot.
As well intentioned as this effort may be, President of Convention of States Mark Meckler could not convince me to support it and refuses to publicly debate me to this day. Why?
Because it sounds good in theory but the reality is it will not work as they claim in the real world of politics which I know all too well.
I have upmost respect for President of Convention of States Mark Meckler and Mike Farris, but neither one despite their best efforts could convince me this is a good idea. I've become an expert on conventions and actually participated and consulted on them. You cannot convince me this is a good idea.
Please call your Republican representative and tell them not to support Convention of States.
Maybe if you elected more Congressmen and women like Bob Good, Matt Gaetz and Lauren Boebert, we would see more real change.

Thanks to SB 815 Small businesses with less than 5 car won't get stuck with cars that are lemons.
Here's the bill language: [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?ses=231...](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001O_3xN4dqBUhZuAeTJmlhpeRKnEFt51eylFBmgLz9tfxz2wmMLS1LtD_WXRbCkxMLCx7_dY_Xxpxfh7aDxYDDx5rDYBrn-AK2XyidpMUczy4hmFc44COhlgUrlxifvCpuLAOcClcY68n-wyQGmdSHyYul1vWlJNpzN-TJYBgDUmEs12oKu_jDwg1frH9bX2g_PEHCCICI9cf976bSXN6mwivz3C_yTXT6gyD38bxTLLt4hCWzG3jhrrOl4VjWANuIbo1ZooK6zbZlOo3BV3HtTEPHzWvtfVPEwj4iLVUIxQ9U0bnkcO-IJMxZlrabD3xb&c=Go79gCVtAt9ULHm7nIFkC4to6kXP2kB9LUs6Y97Ce7ZBtS6lPDLA0w==&ch=8dQwsMuU7Buw7806-FRsgF-I1yxU9RVnMo-n-rkJ-w68w7TleYxe6Q==)
Expands the definition of "motor vehicle" in the Virginia Motor Vehicle Warranty Enforcement Act to include a vehicle with a gross vehicle weight under 10,000 pounds that is bought or used primarily for business purposes by a person or certain legal business entities to which not more than five motor vehicles are registered. The bill also adds electronic mail communications to the definition of "notify" or "notification" in the Act. The bill passed the full Senate. 27 to 12.
Based on this legislation SB1216 that just passed I would not recommend riding in an Uber or Lift.
You will only be covered by insurance by the individual driver's insurance and not the same $1 million coverage you would have with a taxi driver. If the driver has crappy insurance and you're in a serious accident, you will be sorry.
Here's the bill. Healthy debate today on SB1216. Just a note with an explanation in case you are watching the Senate in session.
Each bill receives three constitutional reads.
1\. We only debate bills on 3rd read. 2\. We only adopt amendments and substitutes on the 2nd read. 3\. No action is taken on bills on first read. 4\. We also have a contested calendar and an uncontested calendar. If the bill is uncontested it means both Republicans and Democrats had no issue with the bill. The uncontested calendar lists bills that were challenged, many times these bills pass out of committee on a party line vote. 5\. Bills on the uncontested calendar are usually voted on in a block. Any bill can be pulled out of the block and debated by any senator.
Based on this legislation that just passed I would not recommend riding in an Uber or Lift. You will only be covered by insurance by the individual driver's insurance and not the same $1 million coverage you would have with a taxi driver. This is a Virginia State Crime Commission to study juvenile restitution. The resolution fails because it doesn't have the 26 votes in needs to pass. Good news.

SB1026 Suetterlein is a good bill. We need to protect our kids. Companies are buying and selling the data of our kids without parental consent.

Chase sponsored SB1462 and Reeves sponsored SB1194 bill to eliminate the age retirement for our military retirement benefits died or were PBI'd (passed by indefinitely) in Senate Finance this morning at 9am.

Last year we passed a Senate bill that passed and was signed into law that exempted military retirement benefits from exemption. When we tried to eliminate the age requirement, the bills both failed.

SB920 Senator Stuart proposed a good bill that allows retired law enforcement officers to protect our children.
Permits any local law-enforcement agency to employ in any public elementary or secondary school in the local school division, pursuant to an agreement with the local school board, a school protection officer, defined in the bill as a retired law-enforcement officer hired by the local law-enforcement agency on a part-time basis to provide limited law-enforcement and security services to public elementary and secondary schools in the Commonwealth. The bill requires each such school board and local law-enforcement agency to enter into a memorandum of understanding that sets forth the powers and duties of school protection officers. The bill requires the Department of Criminal Justice Services to establish compulsory training standards for school protection officers and requires the collection of certain data relating to the activities of such officers.
The bill died in the hands of Senate Democrats. "We don't want cops in school because more brown and black children will go to jail."

Back at the General Assembly today with information on SB-898 firearms bill. SB1492 is also on the uncontested calendar and gives certain members of law enforcement the ability to carry on Capitol Square. SB1492 adds an exception for off-duty State Police officers and retired State Police officers to the prohibition on carrying a firearm within Capitol Square and the surrounding area, any building owned or leased by the Commonwealth or any agency thereof, or any office where employees of the Commonwealth or any agency thereof are regularly present for the purpose of performing their official duties. SB898 Adds a firearms safety or training course conducted by the United States Concealed Carry Association (USCCA) or by a USCCA-certified firearms instructor, or any firearms safety or training course or class available to the general public offered by a law-enforcement agency, institution of higher education, or private or public institution or organization or firearms training school utilizing instructors certified by the USCCA to those programs that satisfy the demonstration of competence requirement for the issuance of a Virginia resident or nonresident concealed handgun permit. Follow up. Both bills passed with unanimous consent. Here's are the 2 bills I will vote against: S.B. 909 Patron: Favola Firearm transfers to another person from a prohibited person. Provides that a person who is prohibited from possessing a firearm because such person is subject to a protective order or has been convicted of an assault and battery of a family or household member may transfer a firearm owned by such prohibited person to any person who is not otherwise prohibited by law from possessing such firearm, provided that such person who is not otherwise prohibited by law from possessing such firearm is 21 years of age or older and does not reside with the person who is subject to the protective order. Under current law, there is no requirement that such transferee cannot be younger than 21 years of age and cannot reside with such prohibited person. The bill also provides that such prohibited person who transfers, sells, or surrenders a firearm pursuant to the provisions of the bill shall inform the clerk of the court of the name and address of the transferee, the federally licensed firearms dealer, or the law-enforcement agency in possession of the firearm. A BILL to amend and reenact §§ 18.2-308.1:4 and 18.2-308.1:8 of the Code of Virginia, relating to firearm transfers to another person from a prohibited person. S.B. 1167 Patron: Petersen Firearm industry members; standards of responsible conduct; civil liability. Creates standards of responsible conduct for firearm industry members, as that term is defined in the bill. The bill requires any firearm industry member that sells, manufactures, distributes, markets, or intends to sell, manufacture, distribute, or market firearm industry products, as that term is defined in the bill, within the Commonwealth, or that sells, manufactures, distributes, markets, or intends to sell, manufacture, distribute, or market firearm industry products to be used or possessed within the Commonwealth to comply with such standards of responsible conduct. Such standards include establishing and implementing reasonable controls regarding the manufacture, distribution, importation, marketing, and wholesale or retail sale of firearm industry products and taking reasonable precautions to ensure that such firearm industry products are not sold or distributed to a distributor or retailer that fails to establish and implement such reasonable controls. The bill also creates a civil cause of action for any person who suffers harm as a result of a firearm industry member's violation of the provisions of the bill. A BILL to amend the Code of Virginia by adding in Title 59.1 a chapter numbered 56, consisting of sections numbered 59.1-593 through 59.1-596, relating to firearm industry members; standards of responsible conduct; civil liability. 23102513D Passed out of the Senate 21 to 19

| SETTING THE RECORD STRAIGHT Desperate Tina Ramirez There she goes again...
---
| My desperate opponent Tina Ramirez falsely mentioned on The John Reid Show on WRVA that I was "kicked out of the Republican Senate Caucus" when in fact that is NOT true.
I WAS NOT KICKED OUT OF THE REPUBLICAN SENATE CAUCUS.
In fact, I left based on principle. I could not sit under a leader who introduced tax increases and gun control.
The question is. Would she?
Sometimes leading means you have to take a bold strong stand and draw a line in the sand. It's important to me that we elect Republican leaders who follow the Republican Creed. Leaders should be the example to follow.
As a strong 2A suburban mom, I simply could not sit under leadership that introduced gun control. It's that important.
This decision was not made because of personalities or because I didn't like the leader personally, but based on principle. I could no longer in good conscience sit under a leader that I felt did not introduce bills consistent with the Republican Creed. So true to my word, I left the caucus.
I continue to interact and work with my colleagues, including the leader, to successfully represent my district even under a Democrat controlled Senate.
Just this past year, I worked with my colleagues to pass a bill that exempts veterans retirement benefits from state income tax up to $40,000. The leader and many other of my colleagues signed on as copatrons of the bill, which was signed into law by Governor Youngkin. I've also signed on as a copatron to Governor Youngkin's platform bills to support his agenda for Virginia.
It is difficult not just for me, but all of my Republican Senate colleagues with good bills our base would love to see pass, that don't make it out of committee. That's because the Virginia Senate is still controlled in all subcommittees, committees and the Senate floor by Democrats. If your bill makes it out of the Senate, it's a bill that Senate Democrats would have to like as well.
Think about it.
They do not share our values on election integrity, medical freedom, parental education choice and the list goes on. Finding common ground is difficult.
The Senate has been controlled by Senate Democrats 21 to 19 since 2020 and we just lost another seat in Virginia Beach after Jen Kiggans was elected to Congress. We're now 22 to 18.
Most good legislation is killed in subcommittee to include any good legislation that passes the House.
Most of my colleagues have the same issues I do with getting good bills out of Democrat Controlled Senate committees and subcommittees even with complete Republican support. We simply don't have the votes.
Tina Ramirez doesn't understand how the Senate committee structure works because she's never been in the Senate, nor held any elected office for that matter.
Not sitting on Democrat controlled committees has freed up my time and given me the flexibility to follow and speak on important bills that come up in any committee, not just the ones on which I served.
Because Democrats currently control all committees, Republicans have little input on the bills that pass out of committee. Democrats can pass any bill they want out of committee without the vote of a single Republican. And that's in every Senate committee.
I have more time to prepare for session and time to meet with constituents. Once re-elected and a new leader is chosen, I do plan on rejoining the Republican Senate Caucus.
You need experienced leadership who has successfully served and represented the citizens of Chesterfield, Colonial Heights and Amelia for almost 8 years.
You need a representative with the backbone to stand up to leadership when they go off the rails, not another status quo politician who does what they're told and doesn't stand up for our principles when they are being infringed upon.
Tell Tina to stop telling lies about Senator Amanda Chase. Tell her to stop running against Republicans and instead run against Democrats. If she were truly a conservative, she would support Amanda Chase.

clean_body: From the Desk of Senator Chase | | | | | | == | CUT TO THE CHASE WITH SENATOR CHASE Weekly Session Newsletter Week 3 of 7 | | | | | | WHAT HAPPENED THIS WEEK? | WEEK 3: GENERAL ASSEMBLY UPDATE | | | Senator Amanda Chase Senator Chase has represented Chesterfield, Colonial Heights and Amelia Counties in the Senate since first elected in 2015, almost 8 years. She has been rated as the most conservative Senator in Virginia by the American Conservative Union and has a lifetime score of 100% with the Family Foundation, VCDL and A with the NRA. She is also a champion in the General Assembly for pushing back against vaccine and mask mandates, calling for a full investigation into the 2020 Presidential Election, parental rights in education, medical freedom and help for our veterans. | 2023 SESSION WEEK 3 OVERVIEW Dear Friends, After 2 years of outrunning Covid, it finally caught up with me. Sunday night I tested positive for Covid, but thanks to Dr. Sheila Furey I was able to immediately take Ivermectin. By Thursday I was fully back and on the Senate floor voting against bad gun bills. Thanks to quick acting Ivermectin, I only missed 3 days of session. I was able to keep up by watching online as my team held down the office in my absence. One of my bills that died in Senate Health and Education was a bill that would allow all Virginians to have access to Ivermectin by allowing doctors to be doctors without fear of retribution or losing their license. We need to give Virginians more medical freedom, not less. In this week's newsletter, I provide an overview of the third week of the 2023 General Assembly Session and give you the inside scoop on what really happened this week. Look for a weekly update each Saturday morning. In each weekly newsletter, we'll discuss the most notable General Assembly news of the week. I'll give you an update on bills I've sponsored along with how they fared our Democrat controlled Senate, which is now 22 to 18, and highlight other bills that might be of interest to you. I also want to teach you how to participate in the process. You'll learn how to identify where to find the bills that are before the General Assembly and learn how to track them. You'll learn how to comment or speak on bills so that you can engage in the legislative process. With only 46 days to review roughly 2,000 bills, legislation moves very quickly through the process and can be difficult to track and follow even for the most experienced legislative watchdog. I will also let you know about upcoming events, with exciting news about my re-election campaign, which is already well under way this year. Find out how you can help my campaign while I'm busy doing the work of the people. We have plenty of opportunities to volunteer. | GOP EVENTS | CHESTERFIELD GOP BREAKFAST | | | Saturday morning, Mike & I attended the Chesterfield GOP or CCRC Monthly Breakfast at the Wood and Iron Game Day Restaurant and Bar in Chesterfield County with Team Chase. Great to see Congressman Rob Wittman, Republican nominee Leon Benjamin and so many Republicans coming together to sign each others petitions to get our names on the ballot. If you live in Congressional District 4, be sure to get out and vote in the Special Election on February 21 at your regular polling location. It was a packed house with over 23 candidates present and who will be on the ballot this June 20 and November 7. Better Business Bureau President and CEO Barry Moore was the Special Guest. We learned the importance of not getting scammed by con artists. Incredible turnout at the Chesterfield GOP Business meeting Monday night. Thanks to all who came to join and effect change. We need to grow the GOP so we can have more boots on the ground come Election Day and ensure Chesterfield stays red. Thanks to my surrogate speaker Jody who spoke on my behalf Monday night. A huge thanks to everyone who signed my petition Monday night to get my name on the ballot. We need a minimum of 250 valid signatures to get my name on the ballot. SB1017 would have given us permanent daylight savings time in Virginia. I would definitely support this bill sponsored by Senator Richard Stuart. The bill failed to pass the Senate on an 18 to 21 vote. SJ255 enshrines abortion in our state constitution and is a terrible idea. The bill is copatroned by Senators McClellan and Boysko. This resolution came before the Senate Privileges & Elections subcommittee today and will come up in the Full Senate Privileges and Elections next Tuesday 15 minutes after session in the Capital in Senate Room 3. Abortion is not a constitutional right. Passes 3 to 1 to the full Senate Privileges & Elections Committee Senator Ebbin's SJ242 would enshrine Same Sex Marriage in the Constitution through a Constitutional Amendment. This bill also passed out of today's Senate Privilege and Elections Subcommittee and will also come before the full Senate Privileges and Elections Committee next Tuesday 15 minutes after session in the Capitol in Senate Room 3. I will be a NO vote on this Amendment. Thankfully SJ235 failed to pass out of the Senate Privileges and Elections Subcommittee. Many good Republicans have been deceived into believing Convention of States is a good way to rein in our federal government. Unfortunately, in reality it is a Trojan Horse. While I support term limits and balance budget amendments, calling a Convention of States would be the end of our Constitution. Do you really think only conservatives would be chosen as commissioners? It's naive to believe that only conservatives would be able to amend our U.S. Constitution. Convention of States creates appointed, not elected representation, basically creating an unelected shadow government of unelected representatives that are not accountable to the people. I know how Republican Conventions work. They don't work. They are controlled by political consultants who manipulate and control the process. Convention of States, otherwise called an Article V Convention won't work either. Many conservatives think this is a good idea but they can only speak in theory because they themselves have not been elected and don't understand that politics doesn't always work out as we learned in theory in government class. I am one of 4 Virginian Representatives to serve as a commissioner to the first ever simulated Convention of States in Jamestown several years ago. I can speak from experience as few legislators and so called experts cannot. As well intentioned as this effort may be, President of Convention of States Mark Meckler could not convince me to support it and refuses to publicly debate me to this day. Why? Because it sounds good in theory but the reality is it will not work as they claim in the real world of politics which I know all too well. I have upmost respect for President of Convention of States Mark Meckler and Mike Farris, but neither one despite their best efforts could convince me this is a good idea. I've become an expert on conventions and actually participated and consulted on them. You cannot convince me this is a good idea. Please call your Republican representative and tell them not to support Convention of States. Maybe if you elected more Congressmen and women like Bob Good, Matt Gaetz and Lauren Boebert, we would see more real change. Thanks to SB 815 Small businesses with less than 5 car won't get stuck with cars that are lemons. Here's the bill language: == Expands the definition of "motor vehicle" in the Virginia Motor Vehicle Warranty Enforcement Act to include a vehicle with a gross vehicle weight under 10,000 pounds that is bought or used primarily for business purposes by a person or certain legal business entities to which not more than five motor vehicles are registered. The bill also adds electronic mail communications to the definition of "notify" or "notification" in the Act. The bill passed the full Senate. 27 to 12. Based on this legislation SB1216 that just passed I would not recommend riding in an Uber or Lift. You will only be covered by insurance by the individual driver's insurance and not the same $1 million coverage you would have with a taxi driver. If the driver has crappy insurance and you're in a serious accident, you will be sorry. Here's the bill. == This bill passed the Senate and heads to the House where I hope they kill it. A huge thanks to Republican Senator Mark Obenshain for pointing out the dangerous flaws with this bill. Healthy debate today on SB1216. Just a note with an explanation in case you are watching the Senate in session. Each bill receives three constitutional reads. 1\. We only debate bills on 3rd read. 2\. We only adopt amendments and substitutes on the 2nd read. 3\. No action is taken on bills on first read. 4\. We also have a contested calendar and an uncontested calendar. If the bill is uncontested it means both Republicans and Democrats had no issue with the bill. The uncontested calendar lists bills that were challenged, many times these bills pass out of committee on a party line vote. 5\. Bills on the uncontested calendar are usually voted on in a block. Any bill can be pulled out of the block and debated by any senator. Based on this legislation that just passed I would not recommend riding in an Uber or Lift. You will only be covered by insurance by the individual driver's insurance and not the same $1 million coverage you would have with a taxi driver. If the driver has crappy insurance and you're in a serious accident, you will be sorry. Here's the bill. == This bill passed the Senate and heads to the House where I hope they kill it. A huge thanks to Republican Senator Mark Obenshain for pointing out the dangerous flaws with this bill. Debating SJR 229 Senator McDougle encouraged us to require that juveniles that commit crimes must pay restitution. This is a Virginia State Crime Commission to study juvenile restitution. The resolution fails because it doesn't have the 26 votes in needs to pass. Good news. SB1026 Suetterlein is a good bill. We need to protect our kids. Companies are buying and selling the data of our kids without parental consent. Chase sponsored SB1462 and Reeves sponsored SB1194 bill to eliminate the age retirement for our military retirement benefits died or were PBI'd (passed by indefinitely) in Senate Finance this morning at 9am. Last year we passed a Senate bill that passed and was signed into law that exempted military retirement benefits from exemption. When we tried to eliminate the age requirement, the bills both failed. SB920 Senator Stuart proposed a good bill that allows retired law enforcement officers to protect our children. Permits any local law-enforcement agency to employ in any public elementary or secondary school in the local school division, pursuant to an agreement with the local school board, a school protection officer, defined in the bill as a retired law-enforcement officer hired by the local law-enforcement agency on a part-time basis to provide limited law-enforcement and security services to public elementary and secondary schools in the Commonwealth. The bill requires each such school board and local law-enforcement agency to enter into a memorandum of understanding that sets forth the powers and duties of school protection officers. The bill requires the Department of Criminal Justice Services to establish compulsory training standards for school protection officers and requires the collection of certain data relating to the activities of such officers. The bill died in the hands of Senate Democrats. "We don't want cops in school because more brown and black children will go to jail." Back at the General Assembly today with information on SB-898 firearms bill. SB1492 is also on the uncontested calendar and gives certain members of law enforcement the ability to carry on Capitol Square. SB1492 adds an exception for off-duty State Police officers and retired State Police officers to the prohibition on carrying a firearm within Capitol Square and the surrounding area, any building owned or leased by the Commonwealth or any agency thereof, or any office where employees of the Commonwealth or any agency thereof are regularly present for the purpose of performing their official duties. SB898 Adds a firearms safety or training course conducted by the United States Concealed Carry Association (USCCA) or by a USCCA-certified firearms instructor, or any firearms safety or training course or class available to the general public offered by a law-enforcement agency, institution of higher education, or private or public institution or organization or firearms training school utilizing instructors certified by the USCCA to those programs that satisfy the demonstration of competence requirement for the issuance of a Virginia resident or nonresident concealed handgun permit. Follow up. Both bills passed with unanimous consent. Here's are the 2 bills I will vote against: S.B. 909 Patron: Favola Firearm transfers to another person from a prohibited person. Provides that a person who is prohibited from possessing a firearm because such person is subject to a protective order or has been convicted of an assault and battery of a family or household member may transfer a firearm owned by such prohibited person to any person who is not otherwise prohibited by law from possessing such firearm, provided that such person who is not otherwise prohibited by law from possessing such firearm is 21 years of age or older and does not reside with the person who is subject to the protective order. Under current law, there is no requirement that such transferee cannot be younger than 21 years of age and cannot reside with such prohibited person. The bill also provides that such prohibited person who transfers, sells, or surrenders a firearm pursuant to the provisions of the bill shall inform the clerk of the court of the name and address of the transferee, the federally licensed firearms dealer, or the law-enforcement agency in possession of the firearm. A BILL to amend and reenact §§ 18.2-308.1:4 and 18.2-308.1:8 of the Code of Virginia, relating to firearm transfers to another person from a prohibited person. S.B. 1167 Patron: Petersen Firearm industry members; standards of responsible conduct; civil liability. Creates standards of responsible conduct for firearm industry members, as that term is defined in the bill. The bill requires any firearm industry member that sells, manufactures, distributes, markets, or intends to sell, manufacture, distribute, or market firearm industry products, as that term is defined in the bill, within the Commonwealth, or that sells, manufactures, distributes, markets, or intends to sell, manufacture, distribute, or market firearm industry products to be used or possessed within the Commonwealth to comply with such standards of responsible conduct. Such standards include establishing and implementing reasonable controls regarding the manufacture, distribution, importation, marketing, and wholesale or retail sale of firearm industry products and taking reasonable precautions to ensure that such firearm industry products are not sold or distributed to a distributor or retailer that fails to establish and implement such reasonable controls. The bill also creates a civil cause of action for any person who suffers harm as a result of a firearm industry member's violation of the provisions of the bill. A BILL to amend the Code of Virginia by adding in Title 59.1 a chapter numbered 56, consisting of sections numbered 59.1-593 through 59.1-596, relating to firearm industry members; standards of responsible conduct; civil liability. 23102513D Passed out of the Senate 21 to 19 We love to give the warm welcome of the Senate to our visitors in the Senate Gallery. If you're interested in bringing a group Chesterfield or Colonial Heights please reach out to Debbie at debbie@chaseforva.com. Thankfully the Republican House will kill this bill. S.B. 1167 Patron: Petersen Firearm industry members; standards of responsible conduct; civil liability. Creates standards of responsible conduct for firearm industry members, as that term is defined in the bill. The bill requires any firearm industry member that sells, manufactures, distributes, markets, or intends to sell, manufacture, distribute, or market firearm industry products, as that term is defined in the bill, within the Commonwealth, or that sells, manufactures, distributes, markets, or intends to sell, manufacture, distribute, or market firearm industry products to be used or possessed within the Commonwealth to comply with such standards of responsible conduct. Such standards include establishing and implementing reasonable controls regarding the manufacture, distribution, importation, marketing, and wholesale or retail sale of firearm industry products and taking reasonable precautions to ensure that such firearm industry products are not sold or distributed to a distributor or retailer that fails to establish and implement such reasonable controls. The bill also creates a civil cause of action for any person who suffers harm as a result of a firearm industry member's violation of the provisions of the bill. A BILL to amend the Code of Virginia by adding in Title 59.1 a chapter numbered 56, consisting of sections numbered 59.1-593 through 59.1-596, relating to firearm industry members; standards of responsible conduct; civil liability. 23102513D Passed out of the Senate 21 to 19 Here is an update on the gun legislation that was presented, passed or failed in the Virginia Senate yesterday. Information on some important bills coming up next week. See what you can expect and stay informed! If you ever had a question about the moral decay of our commonwealth and country it is for this very reason, we have elected officials that no longer have a moral compass to guide their decisions but instead are more concerned with being accepted by the "woke" left and in doing so have contributed to the advancement of their agenda. Listen as Senator Hashmi clearly states that she is NOT concerned with sexually explicit material in the reading material of your children as long as it doesn't include violence. How far have we slipped as a nation that this is an acceptable remark from a "leader" of our commonwealth? 1/27/23 Senate Update! Most significant bills are coming next week! Here's what you need to know and how you can participate. | | | | | | == | | | | | == == == == | | | | | WITH YOUR GREAT HELP WE DID IT! FIRST PLACE IN FUNDRAISING | | The Virginia State Board of Elections Released the Latest Fundraising Numbers from July 1 to December 31 And here are the results for Senate District 12: WE WON IN MOST DOLLARS RAISED Chase $147,543.99 Sturtevant $132,454.18 Ramirez $61,417.05 WE WON IN MOST NUMBER OF DONATIONS Chase 183 Sturtevant 159 Ramirez 128 . And we did not solicit money from lobbyists or Political Action Committees. While I'm in session for the next 46 days I cannot raise money. Please save your donations until after we gavel out on Saturday, February 25. Meanwhile both of my opponents can continue to raise money while I'm busy working for you. Please hold your future donations until after adjournment on SATURDAY FEBRUARY 25 Please know that your personal handwritten note is coming, along with those who will receive a Chase Freedom hat. Know that I see your name and that I am so thankful for every donation you made and know that I could not do this without you. You will have to wait until Saturday, February 25 after we adjourn to donate again. I am in session from January 11 to February 25. Our next report will cover from January 1 to March 31, 2023. We will have from February 25 until March 31, 2023 or 35 days to raise money before our next report. | Please Sign My Petition to Get My Name on the Ballot | | In order to get my name on the ballot we must get 250 people to sign my petition. This is something I have to do every time I've run for re-election. If you'd like to sign my petition, please see me at church on Sunday in the foyer in between services or attend one of our upcoming events. Thanks to all who have already signed and asked to collect signatures for us. Please reach out to Jody at jody@chaseforva.com or call 804 912 6007. | SETTING THE RECORD STRAIGHT Desperate Tina Ramirez There she goes again... | My desperate opponent Tina Ramirez falsely mentioned on The John Reid Show on WRVA that I was "kicked out of the Republican Senate Caucus" when in fact that is NOT true. I WAS NOT KICKED OUT OF THE REPUBLICAN SENATE CAUCUS. In fact, I left based on principle. I could not sit under a leader who introduced tax increases and gun control. The question is. Would she? Sometimes leading means you have to take a bold strong stand and draw a line in the sand. It's important to me that we elect Republican leaders who follow the Republican Creed. Leaders should be the example to follow. As a strong 2A suburban mom, I simply could not sit under leadership that introduced gun control. It's that important. This decision was not made because of personalities or because I didn't like the leader personally, but based on principle. I could no longer in good conscience sit under a leader that I felt did not introduce bills consistent with the Republican Creed. So true to my word, I left the caucus. I continue to interact and work with my colleagues, including the leader, to successfully represent my district even under a Democrat controlled Senate. Just this past year, I worked with my colleagues to pass a bill that exempts veterans retirement benefits from state income tax up to $40,000. The leader and many other of my colleagues signed on as copatrons of the bill, which was signed into law by Governor Youngkin. I've also signed on as a copatron to Governor Youngkin's platform bills to support his agenda for Virginia. It is difficult not just for me, but all of my Republican Senate colleagues with good bills our base would love to see pass, that don't make it out of committee. That's because the Virginia Senate is still controlled in all subcommittees, committees and the Senate floor by Democrats. If your bill makes it out of the Senate, it's a bill that Senate Democrats would have to like as well. Think about it. They do not share our values on election integrity, medical freedom, parental education choice and the list goes on. Finding common ground is difficult. The Senate has been controlled by Senate Democrats 21 to 19 since 2020 and we just lost another seat in Virginia Beach after Jen Kiggans was elected to Congress. We're now 22 to 18. Most good legislation is killed in subcommittee to include any good legislation that passes the House. Most of my colleagues have the same issues I do with getting good bills out of Democrat Controlled Senate committees and subcommittees even with complete Republican support. We simply don't have the votes. Tina Ramirez doesn't understand how the Senate committee structure works because she's never been in the Senate, nor held any elected office for that matter. Not sitting on Democrat controlled committees has freed up my time and given me the flexibility to follow and speak on important bills that come up in any committee, not just the ones on which I served. Because Democrats currently control all committees, Republicans have little input on the bills that pass out of committee. Democrats can pass any bill they want out of committee without the vote of a single Republican. And that's in every Senate committee. I have more time to prepare for session and time to meet with constituents. Once re-elected and a new leader is chosen, I do plan on rejoining the Republican Senate Caucus. You need experienced leadership who has successfully served and represented the citizens of Chesterfield, Colonial Heights and Amelia for almost 8 years. You need a representative with the backbone to stand up to leadership when they go off the rails, not another status quo politician who does what they're told and doesn't stand up for our principles when they are being infringed upon. Tell Tina to stop telling lies about Senator Amanda Chase. Tell her to stop running against Republicans and instead run against Democrats. If she were truly a conservative, she would support Amanda Chase. | | | | | | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | | | == | Authorized and Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase | | Friends of Amanda Chase | P.O. Box 5811, Midlothian, VA 23112 | | Sent by mailto:amanda@chaseforva.com powered by

body:
We want you to be able to hold onto your Official 2023 DNC Membership Card all year long and know you helped elect Democrats nationwide. | |
---
President Biden reached out. Vice President Harris reached out. And we’ve reached out multiple times about pitching in $10 to receive an Official 2023 DNC Membership Card. Hundred of thousands of grassroots supporters donated last election cycle to lift Democrats to victory in 2022. name: Team Kilmer
subject: First end-of-month deadline of 2023:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer
body:
Derek is back on Capitol Hill.
Derek is back on Capitol Hill, representing Washington's 6th district, Peter.

After Republicans' historically long (and messy) House Speaker vote - _marking the first time in 100 years that the speaker of the House election has required more than one floor vote_ - **House Democrats are hitting the ground running.**

While we can expect Kevin McCarthy and the new Republican majority to continue their attacks on Social Security and Medicare, reproductive rights, voting rights - and more - Derek remains focused on fighting for our values in Congress.

Despite the odds, our team is doing all we can to regain our blue majority in 2024. Soon, we will be coming up on our **first** end-of-month fundraising deadline of the new year - and of the 118th session of Congress.**We 've set an ambitious fundraising goal of $10,000 to help Democrats prepare for the fight ahead, and Derek needs you onboard.**

**[Peter: Will you consider chipping in just $10, $5, or whatever you can to help Derek reach our FIRST end-of-month fundraising goal of the 118th Congress? After Republicans' historically long (and messy) House Speaker vote - marking the first time in 100 years that the speaker of the House election has required more than one floor vote - House Democrats are hitting the ground running. While we can expect Kevin McCarthy and the new Republican majority to continue their attacks on Social Security and Medicare, reproductive rights, voting rights - and more - Derek remains focused on fighting for our values in Congress. Despite the odds, our team is doing all we can to regain our blue majority in 2024. **Derek,

I'm honored to once again have the opportunity to serve PA-06.**
We accomplished so much together last year – from improving health care benefits for veterans nationwide to preventing the closure of the Coatesville VA in our own backyard (and much more!) – and I am still filled with the same optimism to bring solutions to our most pressing concerns in the weeks and months to come.
At the moment, Congress is controlled by Republicans whose inability earlier this month to elect a Speaker impacted the job we were all elected to do: serve our constituents. Did you know we were initially told to stop doing casework? In my office, we ignored that. But for the brand-new members, not getting their email accounts or offices set up meant they couldn’t even get started.

That was unacceptable. But despite this, I am committed to working with my colleagues across the aisle to deliver for my community. There will undoubtedly be many challenging twists and turns ahead for us, but I am confident that we will face them together.
**[That's why I'm asking you to stand with me, Derek, and chip in $5 before midnight to become an official Team Chrissy Member for this new year.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57867137/389357825/685235109?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk784-fr-nd&recurring=0&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNWQ1NjZhNy01YjllLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=V0k-e-r3-b24Aaf2XACoVae_qgrsoWEJ1AOzkMIoFOM=)**
| | [JOIN TODAY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57867139/389357827/685235109?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk784-fr-nd&recurring=0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNWQ1NjZhNy01YjllLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=V0k-e-r3-b24Aaf2XACoVae_qgrsoWEJ1AOzkMIoFOM=)

Thank you for your support. I look forward to working with you this year.

There's work to be done.

– Chrissy

name: Jacky Rosen

body:
| | |

**I'm about to ask you to donate $5, friend.** But first, let me quickly explain why your donation today is so important.
As Democrats fight to hang onto our narrow Senate majority, election experts are saying that Nevada could once again determine Senate control next year — _and they're already calling me one of the most vulnerable Democrats on the ballot._
That means I'm already one of the GOP's top targets for defeat, so we need to be ready to fight back against the millions of dollars that will pour into this race from dark money groups and Mitch McConnell's megadonor allies. We don't have a second to waste.
**If we're going to win the tough fight ahead of us and keep both Nevada and the Senate blue, we can't afford to miss a single fundraising goal. But right now, we're on track to fall short of our $25,000 end-of-month goal. ****That's why your $5 donation is so urgently needed.**
**[So please, friend, can I count on you to rush $5 or more right now — split between my campaign and Kirsten Gillibrand's — to close this fundraising gap, defeat the GOP in Nevada and defend our narrow Democratic Senate majority?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57890885/389413289/920832744?&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk3854-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZWQ1MGQ0Mi04NjllLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KtQKA9ydU1iyYE5KyI-eNsn-smHbmJ9F-QdRBDD91Ks=)**

clean_body: | | | I'm about to ask you to donate $5, friend. But first, let me quickly explain why your donation today is so important. As Democrats fight to hang onto our narrow Senate majority, election experts are saying that Nevada could once again determine Senate control next year — and they're already calling me one of the most vulnerable Democrats on the ballot. That means I'm already one of the GOP's top targets for defeat, so we need to be ready to fight back against the millions of dollars that will pour into this race from dark money groups and Mitch McConnell's megadonor allies. We don't have a second to waste. If we're going to win the tough fight ahead of us and keep both Nevada and the Senate blue, we can't afford to miss a single fundraising goal. But right now, we're on track to fall short of our $25,000 end-of-month goal. ## LIVE REACTION - Dems Passing Abortion Bill
## We're LIVE at 10:30 AM
Friend, The Evil Leftist Agenda will be on full display this morning on the Minnesota Senate floor. Democrats are debating and taking a vote on the Nation's most EXTREME abortion bill starting at 10:30 am. A bill that allows abortion with no exceptions, up to the minute of birth. **HF1 already passed in the Minnesota House. An amendment to protect pre-born babies in the third trimester was REJECTED. However, one Democrat joined the Republicans in protecting life.** It only takes one Democrat Senator to join the Republicans and this EXTREME abortion bill is killed. But will that happen today? Rep Erik Mortensen will be joining our **LIVE COVERAGE at 10:30 AM**! Don't miss this one. With the help of you and this grassroots team, we made history last November – flipping four chambers, adding three new Democratic trifectas, and defending every single majority. But we can’t take our foot off the gas now – the stakes are too high.
**State Democrats are facing _four ultra-competitive_ , high-stakes special elections next month – some of which are vital for Democratic control – and the Virginia House and Senate are in play this November. **
**Electing Democratic leaders who will support President Biden’s agenda in statehouses across the country is _critical_ to building a better future in your community.** But it’s up to grassroots supporters like you to own a piece of this fight and give state Democrats the resources they need to go toe-to-toe against their Republican opponents.
As an official member, you’ll be at the forefront of the fight to defeat extremist state Republicans, helping elect Democrats who will work to secure a progressive future in your community. **So can we count you in? This is one of the last opportunities to become an official DLCC member this month!**

But we can’t take our foot off the gas now – the stakes are too high. Electing Democratic leaders who will support President Biden’s agenda in statehouses across the country is critical to building a better future in your community. But it’s up to grassroots supporters like you to own a piece of this fight and give state Democrats the resources they need to go toe-to-toe against their Republican opponents. name: Katie Porter
subject: I don’t “do Congress” the way others do
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
Washington needs to change, and we can do that by electing leaders who aren’t beholden to corporate special interests.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=16641%2E3422442%2EVNOGOr)
I’m abundantly proud to say: I “do Congress” differently than most of my colleagues:
* I don’t take money from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil, Big Pharma, or Wall Street Banks.
* I’m leading the fight to ban Members of Congress and their families from trading stocks.
* When Congress brought back earmarks (which are a way of funding Members’ pet projects) in 2021, I was the only Democrat to oppose them.
* When I’m home in Orange County, my toughest constituents are my three kids. Seriously, they track the bills we vote on and come prepared with the tough (but fair) questions.

**Washington needs to change, and we can do that by electing leaders who aren’t beholden to corporate special interests.[I’m ready to take our fight to the U.S. Senate. If you’re with me, consider pitching in today. ](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230127-1?refcode=EMAF27_1_DOC_20230127_V1_F&refcode2=16641_3422442_VNOGOr&akid=16641%2E3422442%2EVNOGOr)**
Katie Porter

clean_body: Washington needs to change, and we can do that by electing leaders who aren’t beholden to corporate special interests. I’m abundantly proud to say: I “do Congress” differently than most of my colleagues: I don’t take money from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil, Big Pharma, or Wall Street Banks. I’m leading the fight to ban Members of Congress and their families from trading stocks. When Congress brought back earmarks (which are a way of funding Members’ pet projects) in 2021, I was the only Democrat to oppose them. When I’m home in Orange County, my toughest constituents are my three kids. Seriously, they track the bills we vote on and come prepared with the tough (but fair) questions. Washington needs to change, and we can do that by electing leaders who aren’t beholden to corporate special interests. Katie Porter If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: Katie is running for the U.S. Senate because Californians deserve a warrior fighting for them in Washington. Our campaign is people-powered, we don’t take a dime from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil or Big Pharma.

subject: 2022 was our year. Now it’s time to take on 2023.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrea Salinas for Oregon
